assertion_results.text: The plan explicitly references reusing common/src/db/query.rs::apply_filter for comma-separated multi-value query parameter parsing rather than writing new parsing logic (constraint 5.4)

Do NOT write custom parsing logic for comma-separated values -- `apply_filter` provides this exact capability.' Also reuse-analysis.md Reuse Candidate 1 (lines 7-11) explains apply_filter is called directly for filter parsing and SQL generation with no custom parsing code.

Build the join using SeaORM's relation/join API with the entity, rather than writing raw SQL joins.' reuse-analysis.md lines 32-44 (Reuse Candidate 3) confirm: 'No new entity definitions, raw SQL joins, or manual table/column references are needed.'

Each section includes 'What it provides', 'How it is reused', and for candidate 1 also 'Why no new utility is needed'. Summary table on lines 50-54 confirms all three.

Other referenced files (common/src/db/query.rs, entity/src/package_license.rs, common/src/error.rs) are explicitly used as read-only references, not modified.
